title: Xylopia gracilis
taxonomy: Xylopia gracilis is classified within the family Annonaceae, which is a large family of flowering plants commonly known as the custard apple or soursop family. The genus Xylopia, to which Xylopia gracilis belongs, is a group of flowering plants native to tropical and subtropical regions of Africa and Asia. Xylopia gracilis is a species of flowering plant native to tropical regions of Africa, specifically in countries such as Nigeria and the Democratic Republic of the Congo. The classification of Xylopia gracilis is further divided into the kingdom Plantae and the phylum Magnoliophyta. It is an evergreen shrub or small tree that grows in the wild and is valued for its medicinal and culinary uses.
uses: Xylopia gracilis is known for its medicinal properties, which include treating fever and inflammation. In traditional medicine, the plant is used to treat various ailments such as respiratory issues and skin conditions. Its extracts have been found to possess antimicrobial and antioxidant properties, which contribute to its medicinal uses. Furthermore, Xylopia gracilis is used in the treatment of gastrointestinal problems and as a natural remedy for wound healing. The plant's medicinal uses are still being researched and studied for their potential benefits in modern medicine.

parts: Xylopia gracilis is a plant species native to tropical Africa.
